core_mechanism: engineering the dissipative (relaxation) dynamics of an open, driven system so that a desired ordered configuration is the unique stationary attractor / fixed point of the evolution — reaching the target state through engineered dissipation rather than by minimizing an energy, with the coupling geometry (local vs. collective) selecting between a stable steady state and a transient collective burst
brief_reason: The abstract describes preparing an ordered state as the stationary state of a driven-dissipative protocol using engineered local dissipation, and contrasts this steady-state attractor with a transient superradiant burst in the collective-dissipation regime, which is exactly steady-state preparation via a designed dissipative fixed point.
